items.instruction: Does this controller requirement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: Provide radar services only if you are personally satisfied that the radar presentation and equipment performance are adequate for the service being provided.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That requirement appears in 5-1-1a.

items.instruction: Does this scope or responsibility statement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: Radar services are not limited solely to the distance and altitude parameters obtained during the commissioning flight check.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That note appears in 5-1-1.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: Radar returns in your sector become intermittent, and an unexpected outage affects part of the display while arrivals are being sequenced toward your airspace.
items.para_context: Under 5-1-1, provide radar services only if you are personally satisfied the presentation and equipment performance are adequate. Notify the OS/CIC of radar malfunctions or unexpected outages and advise adjacent facilities when appropriate.

choices.text: Stop or limit radar services until you are satisfied the presentation is adequate, notify the OS/CIC, and advise adjacent facilities when appropriate.
Continue providing normal radar services as long as the outage remains within the original flight-check distance limits.
Wait until a pilot reports a problem before notifying anyone about the outage.
Transfer all affected aircraft immediately without notifying the OS/CIC because equipment outages are handled only by technical operations.

items.explanation: Per 5-1-1, adequacy is based on the controller being personally satisfied the presentation is adequate, and malfunctions or outages must be reported to the OS/CIC with adjacent facilities advised when appropriate.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: Two turbojet aircraft in RVSM airspace are opposite direction and likely to merge. They are vertically separated by 1,000 feet, and one pilot reports being unable RVSM due to turbulence.
items.para_context: Apply merging target procedures to specified radar-identified aircraft, issue traffic information when targets appear likely to merge unless separated by more than the appropriate vertical minima, and when both aircraft are in RVSM airspace and one pilot reports unable RVSM due to turbulence or mountain wave, use vectors to prevent the targets from merging.

choices.text: Issue timely traffic information and use vectors to prevent the targets from merging because one pilot is unable RVSM in RVSM airspace.
Rely on the existing 1,000-foot separation alone and do not vector because turbojets in RVSM airspace are exempt from merging target procedures.
Wait until the pilots request assistance before issuing any traffic information because closure rate is their responsibility.
Terminate radar service to both aircraft so they can maneuver visually around each other.

items.explanation: Per 5-1-4, likely merging targets require timely traffic information, and an unable-RVSM report in RVSM airspace triggers vectors to prevent the merge.

items.instruction: Does this example appear in the paragraph's approved examples?
items.question_text: "Traffic twelve o'clock, seven miles, eastbound, Gulfstream 650, one seven thousand."

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That traffic-information example appears in 5-1-4b.

items.instruction: Does this minima or separation statement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: When both aircraft are in RVSM airspace, vertically separated by 1,000 feet, and either pilot reports unable RVSM due to turbulence or mountain wave, use vectors to prevent the targets from merging.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That rule appears in 5-1-4c.

items.instruction: Does this controller requirement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: Provide radar surveillance of outer fix holding pattern airspace areas shown on your radar scope whenever aircraft are holding there.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That requirement appears in 5-1-5.

items.instruction: Is this directive stated correctly?
items.question_text: Assist an aircraft to return to the assigned airspace if you detect it straying outside the holding pattern area.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That direction appears in 5-1-5.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: You are providing surveillance for an outer fix holding area shown on your scope. An aircraft holding there starts drifting outside the depicted airspace.
items.para_context: Under 5-1-5, provide radar surveillance of displayed outer fix holding pattern airspace whenever aircraft are holding there, attempt to detect aircraft that stray outside the area, and assist them to return to the assigned airspace.

choices.text: Assist the aircraft back to the assigned airspace after detecting the deviation from the holding area.
Wait until the aircraft exits the entire sector before taking action because holding-pattern protection ends at the charted boundary.
Discontinue surveillance because once an aircraft strays outside the holding area the paragraph no longer applies.
Transfer the aircraft to the next sector immediately and let that controller correct the deviation.

items.explanation: Per 5-1-5, controllers attempt to detect aircraft straying outside the holding area and assist them back to the assigned airspace.

items.instruction: One word in this controller-to-controller phraseology is incorrect. Tap it.
items.explanation: Per 5-4-3c, the correct acknowledgment for a point out is 'POINT OUT APPROVED.' 'TRAFFIC OBSERVED' is a different response.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: You are making a verbal handoff and need to relay all required information to the receiving controller.
items.para_context: Under 5-4-3b, when making a handoff, point out, or issuing traffic restrictions, relay the target position first, then aircraft identification, then assigned altitude and any restrictions or climb/descent information if applicable, and finally any other pertinent information not already contained in the data block or flight data.

choices.text: Relay the target position first, then the aircraft identification, then altitude and restrictions if applicable, then any other pertinent information not already available.
Start with any restrictions, then aircraft identification, and omit target position unless the receiving controller asks for it.
Give the aircraft call sign first, then any pertinent information, and state position only after the point out is approved.
Use whatever order is most convenient, because 5-4-3 does not prescribe one for controller-to-controller coordination.

items.explanation: Per 5-4-3b, the prescribed order is position, identification, altitude or restrictions if applicable, then any other pertinent information.

items.instruction: Place these coordination items in the correct order.

steps.text: State the target position relative to a fix, map symbol, or known radar target.
State the assigned altitude, applicable restrictions, and climb or descent information if applicable.
Provide any other pertinent information not already contained in the data block or available flight data.

items.explanation: Per 5-4-3b, controller-to-controller coordination is relayed in the order of position, identification, altitude or restrictions if applicable, then other pertinent information.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: You are working an en route point out using automated approval in lieu of verbal coordination, but the receiving controller takes no action while the aircraft is approaching the delegated airspace.
items.para_context: Under 5-4-7a1(a), en route automated approval may be used in lieu of verbal approval for a point out, but if the receiving controller takes no action, you must revert to verbal procedures. Approval is required before permitting the aircraft to enter the receiving controller's delegated airspace.

choices.text: Revert to verbal procedures and obtain approval before allowing the aircraft to enter the receiving controller's delegated airspace.
Assume the lack of response is approval and continue because the point out was sent electronically.
Transfer communications and let the receiving controller decide later whether to approve the point out.
Cancel radar service so the aircraft may cross the boundary without point out approval.

items.explanation: Per 5-4-7a1(a), if the receiving controller takes no action on an automated en route point out, revert to verbal procedures; approval is still required before the aircraft enters delegated airspace.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: A point out has already been approved, and you now need to change the aircraft's altitude before it enters the receiving controller's delegated airspace.
items.para_context: Under 5-4-7a2, after a point out has been approved, the transferring controller must obtain the receiving controller's approval before making any changes to the aircraft's flight path, altitude, speed, or data block information.

choices.text: Obtain the receiving controller's approval before changing the altitude.
Issue the altitude change immediately because the point out approval already covers any later change you make.
Change the altitude in the data block and advise the receiving controller only if the pilot reads it back incorrectly.
Wait until the aircraft is inside the receiving controller's delegated airspace and then change the altitude without coordination.

items.explanation: Per 5-4-7a2, once a point out has been approved, later changes to flight path, altitude, speed, or data block information still require the receiving controller's approval.

items.instruction: Does this controller requirement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: After a point out has been approved, you must obtain the receiving controller's approval before changing the aircraft's flight path, altitude, speed, or data block information.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That requirement appears in 5-4-7a2.

items.instruction: Does this scope or responsibility statement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: The receiving controller is responsible for separation between point out aircraft and other aircraft for which the receiving controller has separation responsibility.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That responsibility appears in 5-4-7b2.

items.instruction: Does this scope or responsibility statement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: This equipment does not relieve the controller of proper identification, maintenance of identity, handoff of the correct target associated with the alphanumeric data, and separation of aircraft.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That statement is the substance of 5-14-2.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: The alphanumeric display shows a target association, but two nearby targets make the handoff ambiguous and separation is tightening.
items.para_context: Under 5-14-2, the equipment does not relieve the controller of proper identification, maintenance of identity, handoff of the correct target associated with the alphanumeric data, or separation of aircraft.

choices.text: Resolve the target identification and hand off the correct target while continuing to ensure separation.
Assume the alphanumeric association is enough and transfer responsibility without further verification.
Treat the equipment display as a substitute for separation responsibility and wait for the receiving controller to resolve it.
Ignore the ambiguity because the equipment now owns maintenance of identity once the data block is displayed.

items.explanation: Per 5-14-2, the equipment aids the controller but does not remove responsibility for correct identification, handoff, or separation.

items.instruction: The controller issued a time restriction at a fix. Which pilot readback is correct?
items.clearance: CROSS DULUTH VORTAC AT OR BEFORE ONE FIVE FOUR ZERO.

choices.text: Cross Duluth VORTAC at or before one five four zero.
Cross Duluth VORTAC at or after one five four zero.
Hold at Duluth VORTAC until one five four zero.
Cross Duluth VORTAC at exactly one five zero four zero.

items.explanation: Per 6-4-1, longitudinal separation may be applied by requiring an aircraft to arrive at a fix at a specified time, using phraseology such as 'CROSS (fix) AT OR BEFORE (time).'

items.instruction: One word in this phraseology is incorrect. Tap it.
items.original_phrase: CROSS DULUTH VORTAC AT OR BEFORE ONE FIVE FOUR ZERO.
items.display_text: CROSS DULUTH VORTAC AT OR AFTER ONE FIVE FOUR ZERO
items.explanation: Per 6-4-1, 'at or before' and 'at or after' have different meanings. The displayed clearance changes the required crossing time.

items.instruction: Does this controller requirement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: Separate aircraft longitudinally by requiring an aircraft to arrive at a fix at a specified time.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That method appears in 6-4-1b.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: You need longitudinal separation between two aircraft on the same route, and time control at the next fix is practical.
items.para_context: Under 6-4-1, longitudinal separation may be applied by requiring an aircraft to depart at a specified time, arrive at a fix at a specified time, hold at a fix until a specified time, or change altitude at a specified time or fix.

choices.text: Assign a time restriction at the fix, such as crossing it at or before or at or after a specified time.
Rely on pilot visual separation even if it has not been established or approved.
Use any unpublished crossing-time phraseology because the exact form is left to controller preference.
Delay all action until the trailing aircraft reports the lead aircraft in sight.

items.explanation: Per 6-4-1, a fix crossing time is one of the listed ways to apply longitudinal separation.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: An IFR aircraft executes a go-around from a visual approach at an airport with an operating control tower. You direct it to proceed as otherwise instructed by ATC rather than reenter the traffic pattern.
items.para_context: Under 7-4-1a2, when an aircraft executing a go-around from a visual approach is directed to proceed as otherwise instructed by ATC, the pilot is expected to comply with assigned instructions and maintain terrain and obstruction avoidance until reaching an ATC assigned altitude. ATC must provide instructions to facilitate a climb to the minimum altitude for instrument operations and must provide approved separation or visual separation from other IFR aircraft.

choices.text: Provide instructions that facilitate a climb to the minimum altitude for instrument operations and provide approved separation or visual separation from other IFR aircraft.
Assume the visual approach clearance remains in effect and let the pilot determine a missed-approach path without further ATC instructions.
Provide no further IFR separation because a visual approach ends ATC separation responsibility once the go-around starts.
Require the pilot to cancel IFR immediately before any additional instructions can be issued.

items.explanation: Per 7-4-1a2, ATC must facilitate the climb to the minimum altitude for instrument operations and provide separation from other IFR aircraft.

items.instruction: Does this scope or responsibility statement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: A visual approach is an ATC authorization used for an IFR aircraft to proceed visually and clear of clouds to the airport of intended landing.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That definition appears in the opening sentence of 7-4-1.

items.instruction: Does this controller requirement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: An aircraft unable to complete a landing from a visual approach must be handled as any go-around and provided appropriate IFR separation until it lands or the pilot cancels the IFR flight plan.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That requirement appears in 7-4-1.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: A non-IFR-qualified pilot enters clouds while receiving radar assistance. Another controller wants an immediate frequency change, a sharp turn with a simultaneous descent, and a rapid series of maneuvers to get the aircraft lined up for an airport.
items.para_context: Under 10-2-9, avoid frequency changes except when necessary to provide a clear communications channel. Make turns while the aircraft is in VFR conditions so it can fly straight while in IFR conditions. Have the pilot lower the landing gear and slow to approach speed while in VFR conditions. Avoid requiring a climb or descent while in a turn if the pilot is in IFR conditions. Avoid abrupt maneuvers and vector the aircraft to VFR conditions.

choices.text: Minimize frequency changes, avoid abrupt maneuvering, avoid requiring climb or descent while turning in IFR conditions, and use vectors aimed at reaching VFR conditions.
Use any maneuver combination that gets the aircraft to an airport quickly because the rule puts speed ahead of smooth control inputs.
Require the aircraft to keep turning and descending in the clouds because continuous maneuvering is preferred when the pilot is not instrument qualified.
Shift the aircraft to any available frequency first because communication continuity is less important than distributing workload.

items.explanation: Per 10-2-9, keep communications stable when possible, make turns while still in VFR if feasible, avoid abrupt maneuvers, avoid climb or descent while in a turn in IFR conditions, and vector toward VFR conditions.

items.instruction: Which is one listed radar-assistance technique in this paragraph?
items.question_text: Under 10-2-9, which is one listed radar-assistance technique for a pilot not qualified to operate in IFR conditions?

choices.text: Vector the aircraft to VFR conditions.
Use abrupt maneuvering so the aircraft reaches an airport as quickly as possible.
Delay gear extension until after the aircraft is established in IFR conditions.
Prefer radio frequency changes whenever another controller is available to help.

items.explanation: Vectoring the aircraft to VFR conditions is one of the listed techniques in 10-2-9.

items.instruction: Does this controller requirement match the paragraph?
items.question_text: Avoid requiring a climb or descent while in a turn if the pilot is in IFR conditions.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That requirement appears in 10-2-9d.

items.instruction: What should you do?
items.situation: A terminal controller hears a strong ELT signal that appears to be coming from the airport area. Another controller wants to leave the position to search the field personally and says the ARTCC and technical operations can be notified after more precise bearings are collected.
items.para_context: Under 10-2-10, in terminal facilities notify the ARTCC, which coordinates with the RCC, attempt to obtain fixes or bearings, solicit assistance of other aircraft, and forward fixes, bearings, and other information to the ARTCC. If the signal appears to originate from an airport or vicinity, notify technical operations as well. Air traffic personnel must not leave required duty stations to locate an ELT signal source.

choices.text: Notify the ARTCC, obtain and forward fixes or bearings, notify technical operations if the source appears local to the airport, and remain at the duty station rather than leaving to search for the ELT.
Delay notification until enough bearings are collected to identify the source exactly because outside agencies should be called only after local confirmation.
Leave the position briefly to search the airport because locating the source is more important than remaining at the duty station during an ELT event.
Handle the event entirely within the terminal facility unless another aircraft reports hearing the signal too.

items.explanation: Per 10-2-10, terminal facilities notify the ARTCC, gather and forward ELT information, notify technical operations when the source appears to be at the airport or nearby, and ATC personnel must not leave required duty stations to locate the source.

items.instruction: Is this directive stated correctly?
items.question_text: Air traffic personnel must not leave their required duty stations to locate an ELT signal source.

choices.text: True
False

items.explanation: That directive appears in 10-2-10h.

items.instruction: Which is one listed terminal action in this paragraph?
items.question_text: Under 10-2-10, which is one required terminal action when an ELT signal is heard or reported?

choices.text: Attempt to obtain fixes or bearings on the signal.
Wait for the RCC to provide a fix before taking any local action.
Suspend all coordination until the source is confirmed by technical operations.
Leave the position to search the airport if the traffic load permits.

items.explanation: Attempting to obtain fixes or bearings is listed in 10-2-10c.

items.question_text: Under 5-1-1, what must exist before radar services are provided?
items.explanation: Per 5-1-1a, radar services may be provided only if the controller is personally satisfied that the radar presentation and equipment performance are adequate for the service being provided.

choices.text: The controller must be personally satisfied that the radar presentation and equipment performance are adequate for the service being provided.
The facility must be inside the original commissioning flight-check distance limits regardless of current presentation quality.
At least one adjacent facility must agree the display looks usable.
Pilots must report that their airborne weather radar shows no abnormalities.

items.question_text: When radar malfunctions or unexpected outages occur under 5-1-1, what notification is required?
items.explanation: Per 5-1-1b, notify the OS/CIC of any radar malfunctions or unexpected outages and advise adjacent facilities when appropriate.

choices.text: Notify the OS/CIC and advise adjacent facilities when appropriate.
Notify only technical operations because operational supervisors are informed later through maintenance channels.
Notify pilots first and wait to tell the OS/CIC until the outage exceeds flight-check limits.
Notify adjacent facilities only after the outage has been corrected.

items.question_text: Under 5-1-1, the provision of radar services is limited to the distance and altitude parameters obtained during the commissioning flight check.
items.explanation: The note in 5-1-1 says radar services are not limited to the distance and altitude parameters obtained during the commissioning flight check.

choices.text: True
False

items.question_text: Under 5-1-5, when must radar surveillance of an outer fix holding pattern airspace area be provided?
items.explanation: Per 5-1-5, provide radar surveillance of displayed outer fix holding pattern airspace areas whenever aircraft are holding there.

choices.text: Whenever aircraft are holding in the displayed outer fix holding pattern airspace area.
Only when a pilot requests special holding-pattern monitoring.
Only when the holding fix is outside approach control airspace.
Only when the aircraft are above the minimum holding altitude.

items.question_text: If an aircraft strays outside the holding pattern area under 5-1-5, what must the controller do?
items.explanation: Per 5-1-5, if you detect an aircraft straying outside the area, assist it to return to the assigned airspace.

choices.text: Assist the aircraft to return to the assigned airspace.
Terminate surveillance because the aircraft has left the protected holding area.
Wait for the pilot to request help before taking any action.
Transfer the aircraft to the next facility immediately without further assistance.

items.question_text: Under 5-1-5, controllers should attempt to detect aircraft that stray outside the holding pattern airspace area.
items.explanation: That instruction appears directly in 5-1-5.

choices.text: True
False

items.question_text: Under 5-14-2, what does this equipment not relieve the controller of?
items.explanation: Per 5-14-2, the equipment does not relieve the controller of proper identification, maintenance of identity, handoff of the correct target associated with the alphanumeric data, and separation of aircraft.

choices.text: Proper identification, maintenance of identity, handoff of the correct target associated with the alphanumeric data, and separation of aircraft.
Only verbal coordination with adjacent sectors because the equipment assumes the separation function.
Only beacon-code assignment because all other target-handling duties are automated.
Only recordkeeping, because the display fully validates identification and handoff accuracy.

items.question_text: Under 5-14-2, this equipment relieves the controller of separation responsibility once the alphanumeric data is associated with the target.
items.explanation: 5-14-2 says the equipment does not relieve the controller of separation of aircraft.

choices.text: True
False

items.question_text: Which controller responsibility is specifically named in 5-14-2 in connection with alphanumeric data?
items.explanation: Per 5-14-2, the controller remains responsible for handoff of the correct target associated with the alphanumeric data.

choices.text: Handoff of the correct target associated with the alphanumeric data.
Automatic transfer of communications without controller verification.
Pilot acceptance of any displayed target association without challenge.
Delegation of target identity maintenance to the receiving controller before the handoff is completed.
